questions.questionText: Same volumes of 10% (w/v) $\mathrm{H_2SO_4}$ solution and 10% (w/v) NaOH solution are mixed together. The resulting solution will be
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: When $m_A$ gram of a metal A is placed in the salt solution of another metal B, it liberates $m_B$ gram of B. If the equivalent weights of A and B are $E_A$ and $E_B$ respectively, then the equivalent weight of A is given by :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
A certain volume of hydroxyl amine (NH2OH) solution was boiled with an excess of FeCl3 solution to cause the reduction of Fe3+ ions according to the reaction:

Fe3+ + NH2OH → Fe2+ + N2O + H+ + H2O

The resulting solution was estimated for Fe2+ ions with 0.5M K2Cr2O7 solution in acidic medium. If the volume of K2Cr2O7 solution and NH2OH solution used are found to be equal, what is the molarity of NH2OH solution?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: 0.5 g fuming sulphuric acid (oleum) is diluted with water. This solution is completely neutralized by 26.7 ml of 0.4 N NaOH. Find the percentage of free SO3 in the sample solution.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: One litre of a solution contains 18.9 g of HNO3 and one litre of another solution contains 3.2 g of NaOH. In what volume ratio must these solutions be mixed to obtain a neutral solution?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Which of the following solutions will exactly oxidize 25 mL of an acid solution of 0.1 M iron (II) oxalate :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: An element A in a compound ABD has oxidation number $-n$. It is oxidised by $\mathrm{Cr}_2\mathrm{O}_7^{2-}$ in acid medium. In the experiment, $1.68 \times 10^{-3}$ moles of $\mathrm{K}_2\mathrm{Cr}_2\mathrm{O}_7$ were used for $3.36 \times 10^{-3}$ moles of ABD. The new oxidation number of A after oxidation is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
A 0.2 g sample containing copper (II) was analysed iodometrically, where copper(II) is reduced to copper (I) by iodide ions.

$2\text{Cu}^{2+} + 4\text{I}^- \longrightarrow 2\text{CuI} + \text{I}_2$

If 20 mL of 0.1 M Na$_2$S$_2$O$_3$ solution is required for titration of the liberated iodine, then the percentage of copper in the sample will be :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The amount of lime, Ca(OH)2 required to remove the hardness in 60 L of pond water containing 1.62 mg of calcium bicarbonate per 100 ml of water, will be :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The hardness of a water sample (in terms of equivalents of CaCO3) containing $10^{-3}\ \mathrm{M}\ \mathrm{CaSO}_4$ is: (molar mass of CaSO4 = 136 g mol-1)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The volume of $3 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{Ba}(\mathrm{OH})_2$ solution required to neutralize completly 120 mL of $1.5 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_4$ solution is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Potassium acid oxalate $\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{O}_4 .3 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{O}_4 .4 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ can be oxidized by $\mathrm{MnO}_4{ }^{-}$in acid medium. Calculate the volume of (in mL ) $1 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{KMnO}_4$ reacting in acid solution with 5.08 gram of the acid oxalate.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: In acidic medium, 28 mL of an aqueous $\mathrm{H}_2\mathrm{O}_2$ solution is completely oxidised by 10 mL of $0.1\,M$ aqueous $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$. Calculate the volume strength of the given $\mathrm{H}_2\mathrm{O}_2$ solution.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: A pure sample of $\mathrm{KHC}_2 \mathrm{O}_4 \cdot \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{O}_4 \cdot 2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ needs 30 mol of NaOH for complete titration. In acidic medium, how many moles of $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$ will react with the same sample?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: In alkaline medium, one mole of KI reduces how many moles of $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$?
questions.subject: Chemistry
